data.body:
[G4 child-consistency audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine) × child B5 (f12d: Cut the claim/close gates over to the workflow path, signing intact)]

Independently re-verified all 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster (B1 723c, B2 6b06, B3 dc58, B4 101a, B6 7e48, B-RETIRE 961b, B-DM 5506, B-UX 92fa, 0408). ONE high-confidence finding (conflicting assumptions + scope overlap, B5↔0408), corroborating the prior G4 audit (1782509426618990001) on this pairing. The other six modes read benign (footnoted).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
FINDING 1 — Conflicting assumptions + scope overlap: B5 (this child) is CLOSED + SIGNED asserting `verify.gate_engine` defaults to "workflow" with a live-correct plan-review cutover, but the LIVE tree has the default reverted to "bespoke" (PR #42) because B5's own cutover left the workflow plan-review verify/coach steps without `{{plan}}` on the LIVE path (→ INDETERMINATE). Sibling 0408 (OPEN) silently re-owns the same default=workflow end-state that B5 attests as DONE.
─────────────────────────────────────────────
- finding: B5's signed AC1/end-state ("dispatch through the engine workflow path … DEFAULTING to the workflow path") is not true on the live tree for plan-review; B5 over-scoped its signed verdict (set default=workflow on an unsound live path, then PR #42 reverted it), and the corrective is owned by a separate still-open sibling (0408) without B5 being annotated/reopened.
- criteria: [parent AC4 ("the bespoke orchestrator path is cut over to the workflow path … DEFAULTING to the workflow path; the close/claim gates keep working"); parent Success Criterion ("plan-review … run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate"); B5 AC1 ("…dispatch through the engine workflow path behind a config flag, defaulting to the workflow path"); 0408 AC4 ("verify.gate_engine default is flipped back to workflow") + 0408 AC2 (live review-plan returns a real PASS/BLOCK via the workflow engine)]
- location: src/rebar/config.py:276–283 (gate_engine default); the workflow plan-review verify/coach prompt steps (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py RunnerAgentStep; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml); bespoke listing builders in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py (pass2_verify/pass4_coach).
- evidence: ["B5's signed completion-verification PASS comment asserts 'src/rebar/config.py:276: gate_engine: str = \"workflow\" is the default' and 'AC1 … defaulting to workflow … MET.'", "Live tree CONTRADICTS that: config.py:283 reads `gate_engine: str = \"bespoke\"`, and the docstring (config.py:276–282) states the workflow plan-review verify 'degrades to INDETERMINATE live — a B2 offline blind spot the B5 cutover surfaced,' and flipping to 'workflow' is 'gated on the plan-review plumbing fix (follow-up).'", "gate_dispatch.gate_engine() (gate_dispatch.py:35–44) documents default 'workflow' but config's actual VerifyConfig default is 'bespoke' (config.py:283) — and the unit test test_gate_engine_cutover.py:27 'test_gate_engine_defaults_to_bespoke' pins bespoke, contradicting B5's signed 'default=workflow' claim.", "RunnerAgentStep supplies only {ticket_id, ticket_context, repo_path} to resolve_prompt; the plan-review verifier/coach prompts require `{{plan}}`+findings/surviving listing (per passes.py + 0408 Why), so the live plan-review workflow degrades to INDETERMINATE.", "0408 Why + B-RETIRE comment 1782505678304851001: 'PR #42 had to flip the DEFAULT gate_engine back to bespoke … bespoke is currently the ONLY working plan-review gate.'", "B5's own verifier hedged: 'No full end-to-end live signing test over the workflow path exists' — the offline/structural verification that signed B5 could not see the live blind spot."]
- scenarios: ["A reviewer reconciling the roster sees B5 SIGNED-complete for 'default = workflow / live gate cut over,' yet 0408 (open) says that exact deliverable is broken and must be re-done — two siblings own the same config-default end-state with contradictory completion status.", "If B5's signature is trusted and 0408 is deprioritized/dropped, the epic ships with gate_engine=bespoke permanently — plan-review NEVER actually runs on the workflow substrate live, silently defeating the parent's central Success Criterion despite a green/signed sibling.", "B-RETIRE (961b) removes the bespoke run_review path + the B5 fallback flag; B5's signed evidence would suggest the workflow path is the proven sole-capable gate, tempting premature bespoke removal that would leave NO working plan-review gate (B-RETIRE already re-gated on 0408 manually — but only because the inconsistency was caught by hand, not because B5's face reflects the reversion)."]
- impact: The sibling set is internally inconsistent on a load-bearing claim. B5's signed AC1/end-state ("workflow is the working default") is not true in the tree; 0408 silently re-owns the corrective without B5 being annotated/reopened. The epic's primary Success Criterion (plan-review running live on the one workflow substrate) hinges entirely on 0408 landing, yet a signed sibling already attests it done. Note: B5's COMPLETION-verification cutover IS live-correct (the completion-verifier prompt needs only {{ticket_id}}+{{ticket_context}}, both supplied; signing is byte-compatible by construction) — only the PLAN-REVIEW half is contradicted. This is a real ordering/overlap artifact, not duplication of independent work.
- checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes … conflicting assumptions … scope overlap … each detected mode is a finding."
- suggested_fix: (a) Annotate B5 (f12d) that its AC1 'defaulting to workflow' held only structurally/offline and was reverted by PR #42 pending 0408 — its signed default=workflow claim no longer reflects the tree; (b) surface the 0408→f12d `discovered_from` link as a forward pointer on B5's face, and state in 0408 that it COMPLETES B5's AC1 on the live path (verify/coach {{plan}}+findings plumbing) rather than as an independent task, so no reader treats B5's signature as proof the workflow gate is the working default; (c) ensure B-RETIRE (961b) keys its bespoke-removal precondition off 0408's completion, not B5's signed status (B-RETIRE already added this — keep it).

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
Modes read benign (no finding):

(1) Implicit shared state — B5 mutates surfaces also touched by B2 (gates/plan-review.yaml), B3 (gates/completion-verification.yaml + the executor terminal-output fix B3 flagged for B5), and 0408 (RunnerAgentStep, gate_engine default, passes.py builders). Sequenced (B5 after B4) and the B3→B5 hand-offs (terminal-arm verdict surfacing; INDETERMINATE-on-outage) are acknowledged on B5's face. The contradictory VALUE of the gate_engine default is captured in Finding 1; the remaining co-edits are ordered, not hidden concurrent mutation.

(2) Conflicting assumptions (other) — B5's completion-verification cutover is live-correct (B3's completion-verifier prompt needs only {{ticket_id}}+{{ticket_context}}, both supplied by runs.py; B5's signed produce_completion_verdict PASS + close fail-closed hold). Only the plan-review half is contradicted — captured in Finding 1.

(3) Dependency gap — B5 correctly depends on B4 parity ("parity must hold first") and consumes B2/B1 (ProductionBatchRunner) + B3 (completion-verification.yaml). B5 blocks B-RETIRE (961b) + B6 (7e48) (deps present: f12d blocks 7e48 and 961b). No missing dependency.

(4) Scope overlap (other) — B5 owns the live cutover; B4 reads its trace; B6 retains the sample; B-DM/B-UX build the editor over the same library. Distinct layers, sequenced. The only true overlap (B5↔0408 on the default=workflow end-state) is Finding 1.

(5) Ordering violation — B5 is correctly after B4 (parity) and before B-RETIRE (fallback/bespoke removal). The sequencing wrinkle (B5 prematurely defaulted to workflow before the live plumbing existed) is the substance of Finding 1, not a separate finding.

(6) Consumer impact — flipping the live claim gate to workflow changes gate behaviour, but interface tests pin REBAR_VERIFY_GATE_ENGINE=bespoke to keep validating the fallback, and signing wrappers are unchanged (byte-compatible attestations; attest.build_manifest is path-independent per test_gate_engine_cutover.py:67–74). The live INDETERMINATE regression IS a consumer impact, but it manifested as the gate_engine reversion already captured in Finding 1 — not reported twice.

(7) Residual references — B5 preserves the bespoke path behind the flag (no removal); B-RETIRE keeps the shared passes/sizing/attest machinery the workflow still uses. No dangling reference to a removed symbol.

ANTI-FP note: Finding 1 is reported as a single high-confidence finding because the live tree (config.py:283 = "bespoke"; the unit test pins default=bespoke; runs.py supplies only 3 vars, no {{plan}}) OBJECTIVELY contradicts B5's signed assertion (config.py = "workflow", AC1 MET), and B5 + 0408 own the same default=workflow end-state with opposite completion status. The remaining six modes are filtered out under the benign-reading rule. Corroborates 1782509426618990001.
